info.title: Webhooks Service
info.description:
Webhooks Service

 **Last update**: 02 feb 26, 10:03 a. m.

tags.name: Configuration
tags.description:
## Execution details

Every webhook is invoked by making a POST request to the configured endpoint. The request body will contain a JSON object with information about the specific event that ocurred.

Additionally, you can configure custom headers in order to include, for example, authentication tokens.

The structure of a webhook request body will be the following:

```json
{
  "service": "string",
  "entity": "string",
  "eventName": "string",
  "metadata": "object",
  "eventDate": "string"
}
```

Where `service`, `entity` and `eventName` are the ones you subscribed to, and metadata is an object that may contain specific information for each event.

For example, for an order that is ready for invoicing, the request body will be the following:

```json
{
  "service": "oms",
  "entity": "order",
  "eventName": "ready-for-invoice",
  "metadata": {
    "id": "d555345345345as67a342a"
  },
  "eventDate": "2021-02-23T21:53:57.938Z"
}
```

## Endpoint validation

When a subscription is configured, a POST request will be sent to the endpoint (including the configured headers) an the following request body:

```json
{
  "setup": true
}
```

You **MUST** respond to this request with a 2xx HTTP Status Code. Otherwise, subscription will be rejected.

### Expected response

Any webhook message will be considered as delivered if the endpoint returns a 2xx HTTP Status Code. No response body is required nor validated.

Any other response Status Code will be considered as an error and retry policy will be applied.

tags.name: Retry policy
tags.description:
Upon delivery failure, the following retry policy will be executed to try to successfully deliver the message:

- 1 minute after
- 5 minutes after
- 10 minutes after
- 15 minutes after (four attempts)

If none of this attempts can deliver the message, it will be discarded.

tags.name: Subscription filter
Messages
tags.description:
To handle webhook messages.

A message is created for each event that occurs and matches against at least one subscription.

tags.name: Subscription
tags.description:
To handle your subscriptions to the different event triggers.

You can configure one subscription with multiple triggers for the same endpoint.

post.description: This function allows services to get every entity setting or a single setting for an entity

get.description: Return the setting of an entity

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The name of the entity that owns the settings to be retrieved

put.description: Update the settings of an entity

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The name of the entity that owns the settings to be updated

post.description:
This API allows to test a Subscription Filter against a Sample Event.

This allows for faster feedback when setting a subscription with a filter, removing the need of generating actual events.

id.name: id
id.description: The unique identifier of the item

MessageFilters.name: filters
MessageFilters.description: The filters to apply to the list

lastStatusCode.description: The status code of the last delivery attempt

SubscriptionFilters.name: filters
SubscriptionFilters.description: The filters to apply to the list

trigger.description: A trigger ID. This will filter all subscriptions that have at least this trigger subscribed.

TriggerFilters.name: filters
TriggerFilters.description: The filters to apply to the list

x-janis-total.description: The total amount of records in the list

x-janis-total-updated.description: The total amount of updated items

x-janis-total-error.description: The total amount of items with error

id.description: The unique identifier of the item

dateModified.description: The date of the last modification

userCreated.description: The ID of the user who created the item

userModified.description: The ID of the user who last modified the item

CustomHttpHeaders.description:
Custom headers to be sent on each request.

**Avoid using custom headers for credentials when possible. Use `security` instead**

ServiceTriggers.description: All the triggers of a service

clientCode.description: The code of the client that triggered the event

service.description: The code of the service that triggered the event

entity.description: The entity for which the event was triggered

eventName.description: The name of the event that was triggered

content.description:
The content of the webhook. This is different for each event that is triggered

For details, see the Webhook section on each service documentation, for example see [Webhook section of OMS](/v2/service/oms#tag/Webhooks).

scheme.description:
API Key in headers Authentication

The `apiKey` will be sent in the `header` header. For example: `x-api-key: the-key`

SecurityConfiguration.description:
The security scheme and parameters of a webhook. This is the secure and preferred way to authenticate a webhook.

**Avoid using custom headers for credentials when possible.**

scheme.description:
Basic HTTP Authentication

Username and password are sent in base64 in the Authorization header. For example: `Authorization: Basic dXNlcjpwYXNzd29yZA==`

NoneAuthenticatorSecurity.description: No authentication **Not recommended, use a secure scheme instead.**

scheme.description:
No authentication.

**Not recommended, use a secure scheme instead.**

OAuth 2.0 Authentication

Access token will be fetched first from `tokenEndpoint` using `clientId` and `clientSecret` in a `client_credentials` flow.
Response **must** contain `access_token` and `expires_in` fields.

The access token will then be sent in the `accessTokenHeader` header prefixed with `Bearer`. For example `Authorization: Bearer <access-token>`.

tokenEndpoint.description: The endpoint used to fetch the Access Token

scope.description: The scope requested for the access token (multiple scopes can be separated by spaces)

accessTokenHeader.description: The header name used to send the Access Token

subscriptionId.description: The ID of the [Subscription](#tag/Subscription) that generated this message

endpoint.description: The endpoint where the message will be delivered

service.description: The code of the service that triggered the event

entity.description: The entity for which the event was triggered

eventName.description: The name of the event that was triggered

content.description:
The content of the webhook. This is different for each event that is triggered

For details, see the Webhook section on each service documentation, for example see [Webhook section of OMS](/v2/service/oms#tag/Webhooks).

eventDate.description: The date when the event happened

lastResponse.description: The last response received from the message endpoint

date.description: The date of the last response

error.description: A possible error message if a hook fails to be sent

responseTime.description: The time taken until the response was returned in ms

attempts.description: The quantity of attempts made to deliver the message

status.description: The Event status. It will be `pending` until it succeeds (`delivered`) or definitely fails (`failed`)

dateDelivered.description: The date when the event was delivered without errors

itemsRetried.description: Message IDs that were successfully retried

itemsError.description: Message IDs that failed to be retried

activeOrderLabelTrigger.description: This setting will be used to determine if Webhooks for Print Order Label is active

activePackageLabelTrigger.description: This setting will be used to determine if Webhooks for Print Package Label is active

activeShippingLabelTrigger.description: This setting will be used to determine if Webhooks for Print Shippings Label is active

name.description: A human friendly name to recognize the subscription quickly

triggers.description: A list of [trigger IDs](#operation/listTriggers) to subscribe to

filter.description:
A filter to apply for each event that is triggered. If an event matches, it is sent to the subscription endpoint. If it does not match, it is discarded.

To remove a subscription filter, set this to `null` or empty string.

See [Subscription filter](#tag/Subscription-filter) docs to learn more about setting a filter up.

endpoint.description:
The endpoint where the message should be delivered'

See the [Webhook Configuration](#tag/Configuration) section for more information.

filter.description:
The filter to test against the sample event.

See [Subscription filter](#tag/Subscription-filter) docs to learn more about setting a filter up.

sampleEvent.description: The sample event to test against the subscription filter

result.description:
The result of the test. `true` means that the filter matches the event, `false` means that it is invalid or it doesn't match.

Check the `message` field for more information.

service.description: The code of the service associated to this trigger

entity.description: The name of the entity associated to this trigger

eventName.description: The name of the event associated to this trigger
